title: Dead Motherboard Revival
issue: Dead motherboard - no power at all
excerpt: Brought a Dell XPS 15 back from the dead with a full motherboard repair and power IC replacement.
problem: Client brought in a Dell XPS 15 that would not power on at all. No LEDs, no fan spin, completely dead. The client had tried a different charger and battery removal with no success.
process:
1. Opened the chassis and removed the motherboard for inspection.
2. Found a short on the main power rail (19V line).
3. Used thermal camera to locate the shorted component - a blown power IC (BQ24780S).
4. Removed the faulty IC and cleaned the pads.
5. Soldered a new BQ24780S IC.
6. Checked for any additional shorts on all power rails.
7. Reassembled and tested - system booted perfectly.
8. Ran full diagnostics (CPU, RAM, GPU, storage) - all passed.
9. Cleaned internal fans and applied fresh thermal paste.
partsUsed:
BQ24780S Power IC
Thermal Paste (Arctic MX-4)
reviewText: Sosa saved my laptop! I thought it was completely dead and was ready to buy a new one. He explained exactly what was wrong and had it fixed in two days. Works better than before. Highly recommend!
